Ad Text:

Are you Futureminded? Let's create what's next.

Power BI Consultant 

At Ascendunt, we help organisations turn raw data into meaningful, trusted insights that drive confident decision‑making. Our consultants sit at the intersection of business logic, data modelling, and visual storytelling—ensuring that every dashboard not only looks exceptional but reflects the true intent behind the metrics.

This role plays a critical part in transforming functional requirements into intuitive, high‑performance Power BI experiences that executives rely on to understand performance, identify trends, and make strategic decisions.

Role Purpose
The Power BI Consultant (“The Storyteller”) partners with Business Analysts, Technical Analysts, and business stakeholders to design and deliver reporting that is accurate, insightful, and visually compelling. This role goes beyond simply building dashboards—it uncovers the narrative behind the data, ensuring that every visual aligns with the underlying business rules, data model, and KPI definitions.

The role is central to translating complex datasets into clear, consumable insights that support operational, regulatory, and strategic decision‑making.
Key Responsibilities

End‑to‑End Report Development - Translate business requirements, KPIs, and data rules into intuitive, high‑performance Power BI dashboards that support real‑world decision‑making.
Stakeholder Demos & Presentation -Lead live showcase sessions, confidently walking stakeholders through insights, logic, and design choices—making complex data accessible to non‑technical audiences.
DAX & Data Views - Build scalable, performant DAX measures and star‑schema models. Understand when to leverage data warehouse views versus DAX transformations to optimise performance and maintain logic integrity.
Cross‑Functional Alignment -Collaborate with BAs and TAs to ensure report logic aligns with documented business rules, workflow definitions, and metric calculations.
Peer Review & Quality Assurance - Rigorously test dashboards for accuracy, performance, and usability—ensuring every report is production‑ready and suitable for executive consumption.
Essential Skills & Experience

Advanced DAX & Power Query -Demonstrated experience with complex time‑intelligence, filtering logic, and M‑code transformations beyond basic aggregations.
Data Visualisation Literacy - Strong understanding of how to select the right visual for the right story—avoiding unnecessary complexity or “chart junk.”
Cloud Environment Connectivity - Proven experience connecting Power BI to cloud data warehouses (Snowflake experience highly regarded), with an understanding of query optimisation and performance tuning.
Public Speaking & Stakeholder Confidence -Comfortable presenting live, handling questions, and maintaining control of the room without becoming overly technical or flustered.
Agile Delivery Mindset- Experience working in iterative delivery environments, participating in sprint showcases, and providing transparent progress updates.
UI/UX Design Principles-  Ability to translate reporting requirements into clean, user‑friendly dashboards that guide users through a clear narrative.

Financial services experience

Job Title: Data Analyst / Reporting / Power BI Developer - 12 months+ contract - Perth
Ad Text:
We are a leading Perth‑based technology consultancy, and due to a major upcoming project with one of our key Government clients, we are seeking an experienced Data Analyst with strong BI reporting skills for a long‑term contract engagement.

We are looking for someone with solid experience in data analysis, operational reporting, and Power BI development. Previous WA State Government experience will be highly regarded.

About the Project:

You will support the development of a structured operational reporting capability for an online regulatory platform undergoing modernisation.

Currently, reporting is performed through manual data extraction from multiple systems and spreadsheets. Your role will be to establish a more structured and scalable reporting approach by:

Defining operational metrics and KPIs

Enabling access to reliable datasets within the analytics platform

Developing Power BI dashboards and analytical outputs

You will work closely with operational teams, the project data architect, business analysts, and the Business Intelligence team to ensure reporting requirements are clearly defined and supported by appropriate datasets.

Essential Experience / Skills:

Experience defining operational KPIs or reporting frameworks

Strong analytical skills with the ability to translate business questions into structured reporting requirements

Demonstrated experience developing dashboards and reports using Power BI

Good SQL querying skills

Experience working with operational datasets and preparing data for reporting or analysis

Ability to communicate analytical insights clearly to both technical and non‑technical stakeholders

Excellent communication and problem‑solving skills

Desirable Skills:

Familiarity with dimensional modelling concepts (e.g., fact and dimension tables)

Experience working with star schema or dimensional models in reporting environments such as Power BI

Experience working with enterprise or regulatory systems

Experience in environments where reporting capability is still being established or evolving

Previous WA Government experience

Main Duties / Role Scope:

Define operational KPIs and reporting metrics

Identify required data attributes to support reporting

Work with the project team to enable required datasets within the analytics platform

Develop a small number of high‑value operational dashboards in Power BI

Support validation and reconciliation of reporting data as the platform evolves

Analyse operational data to identify trends, bottlenecks, and improvement opportunities

Map reporting requirements to available system data and identify gaps in data capture

What’s on Offer:

Initial 12‑month contract, with strong potential for extension

Flexible working arrangements (1–2 days WFH per week)

Large, interesting project with real impact

Perth CBD location

$100–$105 per hour (including super) depending on experience

Please click Apply Now to submit your details. All applications will be handled in the strictest confidence.

Please note: This is a Perth‑based role and is not a fully remote position.

Ad Text:

Demonstrated experience in IT Service Management experience with relevant focus in modern IT operations

Excellent skills on ServiceNow or other leading Ticketing system.

We're building something special. Working closely with our industry partners to reshape insurance for the better, we're people changing insurance.

Ad Text:
The role:  

Reporting to the Program Director Better Hospital Flows project, you will be responsible for analysing, manipulating and presenting data.
The Queensland Government is investing in improved hospital services through real-time data and targeted technology upgrades under the Health Plan Commitment. A key priority action is Better Hospital Flows (GEC124), which focuses on modernising digital systems to improve hospital workflows, patient flow and visibility of bed availability across the state. Delivery of GEC124 is being progressed through two related initiatives:

Project 3a, which focuses on enhancements to the Statewide Management Information System (SWMIS) to improve the timeliness, quality and consistency of core operational data; and
Project 3b, which focuses on the assessment, planning and implementation of a statewide Patient Flow Insights capability that translates operational data into actionable insights.

Government Election Commitment (GEC) 124 – Better Hospital Flows – Project 3b comprises two phases: Phase 1 - Assessment and Phase 2 - Implementation.
This role relates to Project 3b: Assessment and Implementation and hereafter refers to it as the “project”.

In addition to this, you will be responsible for:

Plan and design analyses, extract, manipulate and perform statistical analysis of data using statistical software and prepare reports to disseminate information to meet the needs of the organisation.
Query missing, incomplete or inconsistent data using analysis software, including but not limited to Oracle SQL, SAS, R or Excel and undertake analysis and write queries and reports as required.
Display a wide variety of visual reports, KPI scorecards and dashboards, utilizing programs such as Power BI and Tableau.
Inspect, cleanse, transform and analyse raw data to discover useful information, inform conclusions and support effective decision making.
As required, design and build scalable data models and enabling advanced analytic capabilities that provide meaningful business value through easy-to-comprehend data visualisations.
Collaborate with key stakeholders to deliver relevant business insights based on identified business requirements.
Work independently through unstructured tasks.
Prepare confidential, complex reports, briefings and correspondence and initiate action associated with financial, procurement, human resource and other matters as required by the Program Director, Better Hospital Flows.

Are you right for this role?

To be successful in the role, you will have demonstrated capacity to analyse complex system issues and processes, provide specification/solution development and design and business case development. (NFL: Supports Strategic Direction).

Furthermore, you will have demonstrated:

Demonstrated high level of knowledge and experience in statistical methods and concepts and demonstrated ability to appropriately use statistical procedures and interpret statistical information.
Demonstrated high level of programming, data manipulation and analysis ability, including experience using statistical programming packages to interrogate, extract, and manipulate data, conduct descriptive and inferential statistical analysis and produce figures and tables; and experience with large and complex data sets and relational databases.
Demonstrated high level of interpersonal skills and written communication skills, including ability to consult with personnel at all levels in the organisation, external organisations and professional groups, ability to communicate technical information to a non-technical audience, and report writing skills.
Demonstrated ability to prioritise tasks and to effectively monitor and co-ordinate stakeholders to achieve outcomes and to meet deadlines.
Demonstrated ability to manage and develop members of a professional team in a working environment supporting quality human resource management practices.

Benefits of working with eHealth Queensland:

By joining eHealth Queensland you will work within a fast-paced, challenging and supportive environment where every employee plays a critical role in our ongoing success. 

You will enjoy a variety of benefits, including:

Competitive salary + generous superannuation and leave loading
Flexible working arrangements
Diverse work culture
Career training and development

To apply for this exciting opportunity, submit your resume and a one-page cover letter online today.

Ad Text:

About the Role 

We’re looking for an experienced Senior Finance Manager to join our Group Financial Planning & Analysis (FP&A) team on a 6-month fixed term contract based in Sydney. This is a highly visible opportunity to step into a pivotal Group level role, make an immediate impact, and work closely with senior leaders during a critical period for the business. 

Reporting to the Group Head of FP&A and partnering with another Senior Finance Manager, you’ll take ownership of a fast-paced reporting function that underpins executive decision-making and drives performance across the Group. 

This role offers variety, influence, and challenge — spanning monthly Group reporting, the Annual Business Plan, biannual external market reporting, and ad hoc commercial insights that respond to evolving priorities. Your work will be seen, valued, and relied upon. 

What You’ll Be Doing 

Lead the Group consolidation, analysis, and monthly reporting process, delivering clear, insightful commentary on performance and key business drivers. 

Manage and support FP&A team members (internal and outsourced), while remaining hands on with critical reporting submissions. 

Drive delivery of the annual Operational Plan, including 5 year financial forecasts and Board level reporting. 

Oversee year-end external market reporting and partner with Investor Relations to support analyst and investor engagement. 

Review Group Treasury forecasts and credit metrics, applying strong capital management expertise. 

Contribute to ongoing Finance Transformation and systems initiatives. 

Build strong, trusted relationships with senior stakeholders across the Group and provide guidance and coaching to FP&A team members. 

About You: 

CA or CPA qualified, with 5+ years’ post qualification experience in Management Reporting and/or FP&A. 

Proven experience as a Senior Finance Manager, or a high performing Finance Manager ready to step up. 

Exposure to capital management or experience within one of Lendlease’s operating segments will be highly regarded. 

Strong analytical and data visualisation skills, with the ability to distil complexity into clear, actionable insights.

We are Woolworths Group

We are Woolworths Group. 200,000+ bright minds, passionate hearts and unique perspectives connected by a shared Purpose – ‘to create better experiences together for a better tomorrow.’ It’s that Purpose that fuels our ambition to explore new ideas, make brave commitments and innovate better ways to meet the food and everyday needs of more than 24 million customers every week. 




If you’re excited to turn today’s blue sky thinking into a better tomorrow for future generations, you’ll find yourself supported and enriched in a dynamic, inclusive and empowering workplace that reflects the diverse communities we serve. With a culture of genuine care, a flexible approach to work and opportunities across the group to grow your career and make a meaningful impact, the possibilities for what we can achieve together are endless.




What you’ll do

Reporting to the Store Loss Insights Manager, the Fraud Insights Lead is a pivotal role within the Store Loss team, primarily responsible for leveraging data and technology to identify and mitigate internal and external fraud. To support the business in identifying coaching opportunities and internal/external dishonesty by utilizing advanced technology and data systems. This role packages actionable insights for investigation and store management to drive performance conversations and profit protection.

Identify New Fraud Behaviors: Proactively research and identify emerging fraud patterns and behaviors within the store environment.

AI Implementation: Leverage AI-driven methodologies to automate the detection of suspicious activities and improve the accuracy of fraud alerts.

Data Engineering: Work within the GCP (Google Cloud Platform) to develop and manage new datasets and create sophisticated rules for real-time fraud monitoring.

System Validation: Use multiple systems to validate fraudulent behaviors and quantify their impact on profit, packaging these findings for formal investigations like CCTV, POS,R10 etc.

Collaboration: Drive the overarching fraud prevention strategy by collaborating with internal and external partners to identify and fix systemic loopholes.

Stakeholder Engagement: Partner across Store Support, State Operations, and Project teams to embed collaborative initiatives and incident reduction processes.

Loss Priorities: Contribute to the development of strategic Loss priorities and support the monitoring of controls to prevent incident recurrence

Coaching & Training: Upskill store managers to conduct effective investigations and lead team member performance conversations.

Customer First: Ensure all planning and prioritisation focus on the customer while keeping processes simple and unambiguous for store teams.




What you’ll bring

Deep understanding of internal and external fraud, retail processes and supplier mechanics

Experience creating rules and datasets within Google Cloud Platform

Practical experience applying AI technologies to solve business problems

Proficient in Scan Assist, Auror, March Networks (CCTV), and store front-end systems (1 POS, Store Central, Retalix, Scan & Go)

Proficiency in Google Suite, knowledge of Sheets, Docs and Drive

High-level negotiation and presentation skills, with the ability to gain stakeholder agreement on complex decisions

Ad Text:
Important leadership role helping shape cash-related policy

Impactful work that makes a difference to the Australian people

Permanent opportunity, hybrid working

About the team

We are seeking an experienced economist or public-policy professional to join the Analysis and Policy (A&P) area of the RBA's Note Issue Department.

The Analysis and Policy section develops evidence-based policies to support the Bank's strategic priorities in relation to issuing secure and reliable banknotes, and shaping the future of money. We conduct research on cash use, access and acceptance to understand how the cash ecosystem is evolving and to identify potential policy measures to support cash as a viable means of payment. We also facilitate public-sector coordination on cash initiatives, working closely with Council of Financial Regulator (CFR) agencies and the ACCC.

A&P is leading a number of high-priority workstreams to address challenges in cash distribution.

About the role

As a manager in A&P, you will:


Provide economic and policy advice that supports priority topics and contributes to NI meeting its strategic goals, including the Bank's strategic priority to support access to physical cash.

Communicate effectively to relevant internal and external stakeholders in support of the Bank's functions and objectives.

Provide coaching and development opportunities for analysts, to support them in achieving their goals.

Contribute to the broader strategic direction and leadership of the Note Issue Department, sharing accountability with other leaders for overall performance of deliverables and outcomes.

Foster an open and inclusive culture to enable your team to deliver high quality and impactful work.

About you


Excellent critical thinking skills to guide and produce targeted analysis on complex policy issues.

Strong communication and stakeholder engagement skills.

Demonstrated people leadership skills and ability to nurture a culture of trust and collaboration in line with the Bank's open and dynamic ambition. This includes the ability to develop your team to deliver results and build skills over time.

An ability to write clearly and succinctly, with the purpose and intended audience in mind.

Strong work planning, prioritisation and organisation skills.

An ability and willingness build and maintain a culture of trust and collaboration.

Have a relevant tertiary qualification, such as in economics, finance, public policy or statistics.

Be More

Working for an organisation that truly makes a difference to the people of Australia, we can offer development and career opportunities in a collaborative environment that supports your growth, wellbeing and promotes flexibility. Your individual growth and success drives the RBA forward as an organisation. Be more means you can do more, for yourself and for Australia.

Why RBA?

The RBA makes an important contribution to the Australian economy through the pursuit of national economic policy objectives and associated activities in financial markets and banking. We also issue Australia's banknotes and operate infrastructure critical to the payments system, all of which contribute to the welfare of the Australian people. Made up of specialists across a wide range of fields, our people, values, and culture play a critical role in achieving our objectives. Striving to be Open & Dynamic, we consider and incorporate different perspectives, work across teams and are transparent with each other, whilst delivering quality together effectively and focusing on outcomes by prioritising, testing, learning, and refining as we go. Our people conduct themselves with a high degree of integrity, while striving for excellence in the work they perform and the outcomes they achieve. We encourage intelligent inquiry and we treat one another with respect while promoting the public interest through our efforts. We know it is the growth and success of our people that drives the RBA forward. Come and make a bigger contribution while you build and develop your own skills too, because being more means you can do more, for yourself and for Australia.

The Reserve Bank of Australia is committed to equity, diversity and inclusion through key initiatives. We welcome and encourage applicants from diverse backgrounds to apply, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, culturally and linguistically diverse background, those living with a disability and from the LGBTQ+ community. We are committed to making the recruitment process fair and equitable for all our candidates.

Job Title: Senior Capital & Liquidity Lead - Financial Analytics
Ad Text:
About the role: 

As the Senior Capital & Liquidity Lead - Financial Analytics, you will be a key member of the Financial Analytics team within the Finance & Data Division. You will hold Line 1 accountability for capital, liquidity and balance sheet risk management activities and provide forward‑looking insights that support informed decision making across the Bank.

Your key responsibilities will include:

Lead the development and delivery of the Bank’s ICAAP and Recovery Plan, ensuring alignment with APRA prudential standards and regulatory expectations.

Design and deliver enterprise-wide stress testing frameworks, including scenario design, model development and interpretation of outcomes to inform capital planning and strategic decision making.

Present capital and liquidity insights to Executive Committees and the Board, supporting strategic balance sheet decisions.

Develop and maintain liquidity analysis and metrics, including Liquidity Coverage Ratio (LCR) capability.

Ensuring strong governance, documentation and alignment across capital, liquidity, ALM, FTP and forecasting frameworks.

Providing technical leadership, coaching and knowledge sharing within the Financial Analytics team.

About you

To be successful in this role, you will bring strong technical capability, sound judgement and the ability to influence and engage senior stakeholders.

You will also demonstrate:

Significant experience in financial institutions, with strong knowledge of balance sheet management, capital, liquidity and financial risk management.

Proven experience leading ICAAP, capital management, stress testing or balance sheet risk frameworks within a regulated financial institution.

The ability to design, interpret and explain complex financial models, stress testing outcomes and risk frameworks.

Strong analytical skills, attention to detail and the ability to translate technical analysis into clear insights for non‑technical audiences.

Well developed communication and stakeholder engagement skills, with confidence working with Executives and senior leaders.

A proactive, risk‑aware mindset and commitment to continuous improvement and professional development.

Qualifications

Tertiary qualification in Finance, Business, Economics, Actuarial Studies or a related discipline is essential.

Postgraduate or professional qualifications (CA, CPA, CFA or similar) and relevant industry memberships are desirable.

A workplace for you to be you. 

Come and work with a team that values your unique perspective, contribution, career development and growth. 

Why you’ll love working with us:

People First Bank – our new brand – says exactly what we’re all about: people. We're committed to supporting and growing our team and creating an outstanding work environment.

You’ll have access to financial and lifestyle benefits to support your success and individuality:

Flexibility and hybrid working arrangements.

Extend your salary with discounts on banking, health insurance, and shopping across Australia.

Advance your career with training, study support, and project opportunities.

Competitive leave benefits that empower employees to take time for themselves and loved ones.

Wellbeing support with our Employee Assistance Program, wellbeing leave, mental health app, and mental health ambassadors’ program.

Make a difference with paid volunteering, Workplace Giving, and diverse community initiatives.

Be recognised for your contributions through our peer-driven recognition program.

A bit about People First Bank: 

In March 2023, People’s Choice and Heritage Bank merged to form Heritage and People’s Choice Limited - Australia's leading mutual bank.  We are now on an exciting journey to bring together our products, services, and technology under a single brand - People First Bank.

We have over 700,000 members and 1,900 employees, with dual head offices in Adelaide and Toowoomba, an Australian-based contact centre and branches across five states. 

With a vision ‘To deliver positive change through banking’, People First Bank serves members and contributes to the community, while offering an inclusive, flexible work environment. We’re big enough to make an impact; but agile enough to influence and do interesting work.

The role of the Strategic Insights Lead is to evaluate business strategies, inform product development, and provide analytical support for decision-making that affect the strategic direction, with a focus on improving customer experiences and achieving sustainable outcomes.

The Lead oversees and deliver the end-to-end solution, provide strategic direction, develop robust and compelling content to back up our proposals, and incorporates market analysis and competitive insights to substantiate the evidence presented.

The position involves cultivating a strong understanding of the CEP industry, retail market, generating and disseminating thought leadership, as well as overseeing the budget and forecasts. 

 

What you’ll deliver with us  

Moreover, the position requires extensive stakeholder management, cultivating collaborative relationships both within and beyond Freight Services. The Lead also provides expert guidance to senior stakeholders, assisting in strategic planning and addressing intricate challenges, thus advancing the overarching objectives of Freight Services and adapting to organisational changes.




You’ll also   

•    Lead the assessment of business strategies from the planning stage to implementation. 

•    Lead and manage the strategies to gather, analyse, and interpret data to support business initiatives.

•    Provide decision support analysis to shape our strategic direction.

Experience in developing product strategy and the end-to-end product lifecycle management.

Want to be the change behind the improvement of healthcare?

I'm partnering with San Francisco’s fastest growing HealthTech company that is becoming a category-defining institution in consumer health. 

You will be joining an experienced team of multi-time founders – who have started prior companies worth >$500m+. Their founding team is ex. Amazon, Y Combinator, Harvard Med + many more.

You’ll be given the keys to design, build and instrument a durable, multi-faceted growth engine that drives user acquisition, activation, and retention - on top of an exponentially growing customer base (10 x in the last 6 months alone)!

You won’t be inheriting a playbook; you will be the architect of it. 

Core Problems You'll Own:

Growth Model Architecture: Deconstruct the existing user journey and map out the core loops (acquisition, engagement, viral). You will design the system, instrument it, and identify the key inputs that drive growth equation.
High-Velocity Experimentation: Build and lead the operational cadence for experimentation. You will own the framework for generating hypotheses, shipping tests (from messaging and landing pages to in-product flows), and analyzing results with statistical rigor.
Lifecycle & Retention Systems: Go beyond basic email flows. Architect a compounding lifecycle system that intelligently segments users and drives them toward activation and long-term retention. Every touchpoint should feel like part of a cohesive, premium product experience.
Technical Growth Infrastructure: Partner with engineering to ensure we have the right stack and instrumentation in place. You should be comfortable discussing event tracking, data warehousing, and the APIs needed to power your growth loops.
You! ‘A builder with Technical depth’
You Have Builder DNA: They love anyone with experience as a founder (no matter the result), a founding team member, or an early operator at a startup that experienced non-trivial scale. You are biased toward action and have a palpable sense of ownership.
You're Technically Literate & Quantitatively Rigorous: You are a heavy claude code user and deeply comfortable with technical concepts. 
You Think in Systems & Loops, Not Channels: Your first instinct isn't to ask "what's the budget?" but "what's the loop?" You have proven experience designing and scaling growth loops (e.g., product-led, content, or viral) that compound over time.
You've Operated at Scale: You know what it takes to get from 6 to 7 to 8 figures. You’ve seen the breaking points and understand how to build systems that can handle hyper-growth.
You Have a Strong, Data-Informed Point of View: You have opinions—backed by data and experience—on topics like paid vs. organic, PLG vs. sales-led, and how to balance brand with performance.

* Strong understanding of incident and problem management processes with exposure to ServiceNow or similar platforms
* Proven experience analysing operational data and translating insights into actionable recommendations within enterprise environments

DPS also provides a range of additional project management services to support agency businesses. To deliver the programs and projects, DPS rely extensively on service providers and their capability to source specialist services to meet the specific demands of the program needs. Overview of the project & work unit the position is related to. The Change and Communications Analyst will support the project by delivering a range of change management, engagement, communication and training activities. This includes
analysing and documenting change impacts, developing and facilitating workshops and briefings, and producing clear and targeted communication materials. The role will contribute to engagement, training preparation, and business readiness activities to support stakeholder adoption and transition to business as usual. Strong partnerships with technical and business stakeholders will be maintained throughout the project, along with support for post implementation review and continuous improvement.

Scope of Works
Reporting to the Project Manager, the Change and Communication Analyst will work in a coordinated approach to deliver a number of activities to support the eRostering project. Activities include:

Change Impact Analysis – work with program and business stakeholders to clearly identify and document what has changed between current and future state processes
Change workshops & briefings – develop presentation material and briefings and facilitate engagement sessions with both program and business stakeholders.
Communications - Develop and deliver change communications, including updates, presentations, key messages, newsletters, and briefing packs.
Change activities - support the Manager with communication, engagement and training interventions that enable a successful change journey for stakeholders.
Readiness assessments – identify and deliver activities to enable optimal adoption of changes to current practices, workflows and/or processes.
Stakeholder partnership - establish and maintain strong relationships with technical and business stakeholders.
Support post implementation and review process

Over 2 years’ experience in a Change and Communication Analyst or similar role.

Demonstrated experience in stakeholder engagement and building collaborative relationships to support effective change adoption.
Proven ability to develop high-quality communication materials tailored for diverse audiences and channels.
Ability to apply change management principles, tools, and techniques to support program outcomes.
Experience supporting business readiness activities and executing communication strategies.
Strong stakeholder management skills, with the ability to identify delivery risks and implement suitable mitigation strategies; formal change management qualifications are desirable.

Desirable

Tertiary qualifications in a relevant discipline (e.g., Human Resources, Psychology, Business, or Communications) and/or professional certification in a recognized Change Management methodology (e.g., Prosci, Association of Change Management Professionals, or Change Management Institute) or demonstrated equivalent professional experience.

Personal Qualities Required

Thorough understanding of communication and change analysis principles, methodologies and change management best practices.
A critical thinker with the ability to connect the dots and identify solutions.
An effective communicator (written and verbal) who tailors ideas in ways that are appropriate to the target audience.

Good attention to detail and analytical skills, with the ability to draw conclusions and insights from data.
Excellent prioritisation skills to support an agile product delivery
Well-developed cross-cultural communication, influencing and interpersonal skills with the ability to build and sustain productive relationships.

Job Title: Snr Scientist – Analytical Development (CMC) | Great Place To Work® Certified
Ad Text:
```html

The Company

Starpharma is an innovative Australian biotechnology company located in inner Melbourne. With two decades of experience advancing dendrimer technology from the lab to the patient, our mission is to help patients with significant illnesses, such as cancer, achieve improved health outcomes and quality of life through the application of our unique dendrimer technology.

Through our innovative R&D and strategic partnerships with leading pharmaceutical companies, we are developing therapies that have the potential to profoundly improve patient health worldwide.

Joining the team at Starpharma means that you will be part of a team that is creating medical and pharmaceutical products that have the potential to positively impact people around the world. Working at Starpharma offers the reward of contributing to the broader community. At Starpharma, you will work closely with other talented and committed professionals who share a common focus. We take pride in offering a great culture, professional working environment, attractive remuneration package including an employee incentive program, Health & Wellbeing program, and the opportunity to continue developing your career as part of a small team in the dynamic biotech industry.

Starpharma has achieved Great Place to Work® certification for four consecutive years. This validates that we care about our employees' experience and are committed to building and maintaining a high-trust company culture. Great Place to Work®'s insights enable dynamic, ever-evolving businesses like Starpharma to reflect on their results and continue enhancing their workplace culture. Certification recognises our positive workplace and company culture, and our celebration of inclusivity and diversity in our workplace.

The Position

Reporting to the Director Translational Science & CMC, you will take on a predominantly laboratory‑based senior scientist role, working closely with colleagues across Analytical, Discovery, Development, Cancer Biology, Scale‑Up and Quality & Regulatory.

Note that this is a permanent full-time position and requires a minimum of 7-10 years of experience in analytical method development within the pharmaceutical/biotech sector.

Key responsibilities include:

Analytical development & lifecycle for complex conjugates

Providing senior, predominantly laboratory-based expertise in the analytical development and characterisation of dendrimers and dendrimer-based conjugates (including biologic and radiotheranostic components).

Planning, performing and documenting experimental work, including sample preparation, instrument setup, acquisition and primary data review for methods such as LCMS, HPLC/UHPLC/UPLC, SEC, HIC and other suitable chromatographic or biophysical techniques.

Develop, optimise and troubleshoot analytical methods for complex dendrimer conjugates, ensuring methods are phase-appropriate (fit-for-purpose at early stages, progressing to fully validated as programs advance).

Undertaking analytical method qualification and validation in accordance with current ICH/EMA/FDA guidelines and relevant pharmacopoeial and GxP requirements, including hands-on execution of validation experiments.

Regulatory & CMC integration

Preparing high-quality analytical documentation (method development reports, validation protocols/reports, specification rationales, data summaries) to support CMC sections of regulatory submissions (e.g. CTAs/INDs, IMPDs, NDAs/BLAs) and assisting in responses to regulatory questions on analytical topics.

Working with CMC project managers, Quality & Regulatory and external partners to help define phase-appropriate specifications and control strategies, including impurity profiles and stability-indicating methods for complex dendrimer conjugates.

New techniques & external partnerships

Proactively identifying, from scientific literature, conferences, vendors, regulatory guidance and expert networks, analytical approaches suitable for dendrimer conjugates with biologic and radiotheranostic components and assessing their feasibility for use in Starpharma's programs.

Designing and executing laboratory feasibility studies to evaluate new analytical techniques (e.g. novel chromatographic, biophysical, bioanalytical or radiochemical methods) and, where successful, supporting their implementation in-house or via selected external laboratories.

Providing scientific input into the identification, qualification and management of external analytical partners (e.g. specialist analytical, radiochemistry or complex biophysical labs), including contributing to scopes of work, protocol review and interpretation of complex data packages.

Cross-functional and translational support

Collaborating closely with Discovery, Cancer Biology, Scale-Up and Development teams to ensure analytical outputs appropriately inform mechanism-of-action questions, structure–activity relationships and translational hypotheses for dendrimer conjugates, and to support the smooth transition of projects from discovery to development and GMP scale-up.

Leadership, mentoring and ways of working

Providing informal leadership within the analytical group, mentoring and coaching junior analysts and scientists in hands-on analytical technique, experimental design, analytical development principles, regulatory awareness and scientific problem-solving.

Acting as a change agent within the team, supporting the adoption of new techniques and interpret regulatory expectations constructively.

Quality systems, safety and general responsibilities

Contributing to the development and maintenance of a phase-appropriate GMP-compliant QA system, including writing and reviewing SOPs, protocols, test methods, specifications and reports, and taking an active role in day-to-day instrument troubleshooting and maintenance.

Innovating solutions to analytical problems encountered as well as characterisation questions from both the Discovery and Development groups.

Contributing to calibration and scheduled maintenance of analytical equipment.

Contributing to the continuing development and maintenance of the QMS, including writing and reviewing documents such as SOPs, protocols and reports.

Maintaining detailed records of work and provide clear interpretation of own results both written and orally.

Identifying, analysing and managing risks associated with areas of responsibility and expertise.

Maintaining a working knowledge of current regulatory guidelines related to analytical testing for bio/pharmaceutical products.

Maintaining awareness of and compliance with Occupational Health and Safety legislation

Contributing to risk identification, analysis and management.

In addition to the specific requirements for this role, STAR values and behaviours we look for in all employees are:

Superior performance

Persevering and going the extra mile when it is needed

Effectively prioritising and working with a sense of urgency

Ensuring quality and accuracy

Teamwork

Collaborating and teaming in an effective, solution-focused way with others to achieve desired outcomes

Using a respectful and clear communication style which supports and encourages teamwork across the company and facilitates optimal business outcomes

Having a positive energy, and a "can do" and optimistic attitude for oneself and in influencing others

Accountability

Taking ownership of our actions and decisions

Reviewing situations and decisions to harvest the learnings for future benefit (lessons learned)

Being honest and doing what we say we will do

Being willing to remedy unintended consequences of actions or decisions, where appropriate

Re-imagination

Demonstrating curiosity, being open to and taking the initiative to generate new ideas

Identifying problems and generating potential solutions, taking account of costs/benefits and risks/opportunities through a commercial and strategic lens

Showing flexibility and adaptability

Benefits of working at Starpharma

Highly respected employer – Great Place to Work® certified

Small, committed team

Professional, collegial environment

Competitive remuneration including cash and equity-based incentives

Fantastic health and wellbeing program with a wide range of activities for social, physical & mental wellbeing, plus EAP

Support for professional development including access to high quality online learning resources

Candidate Profile

Applications must explicitly address the following key selection criteria:

Candidates much have Australian work rights at the time of application.

A minimum of BSc with Chemistry or Biochemistry major, and significant experience (typically 7–10+ years) in analytical method development in the pharmaceutical/biotech sector. Alternatively a higher research degree in analytical chemistry or a related field would be well regarded

Demonstrated experience in laboratory‑based analytical development and validation for complex modalities (e.g. conjugates, biologic components, macromolecules, radiotheranostics), including hands‑on method optimisation, troubleshooting and data interpretation.

Strong proficiency with chromatographic and mass spectrometric methods (e.g. LCMS, HPLC/UHPLC/UPLC), and ideally experience with SEC, HIC and/or other biophysical methods relevant to conjugates and biologics

Demonstrated curiosity and willingness to explore and implement new analytical techniques beyond standard chromatographic approaches, including engaging with scientific literature, vendors and external labs as needed.

Working knowledge of ICH/EMA/FDA analytical and CMC guidelines (e.g. Q2(R2), Q6, Q8–Q10, Q14) and relevant GxP requirements (GLP, GMP and, where applicable, GCP), and experience applying these in a phase‑appropriate, risk‑based manner

Familiarity with chromatography data systems (eg Waters Empower or Agilent Chemstation).

Ability to work confidently with proteins and biological fluids. Experience in the analysis of large molecular weight molecules (eg, proteins, antibodies and/or oligonucleotides) is desirable

Previous work experience in a GMP or GLP‑aligned QA‑accredited laboratory supporting drug substance and/or drug product development.

A work style characterized by diligence and a strong work ethic

Ability to keep detailed records and produce high quality reports

Commitment to teamwork, respectful communication and strong interpersonal skills.

Flexible and responsive approach to work including an enthusiasm for acquiring new skills.

Want to apply for this job?

If applying for this role, please include a cover letter that outlines:

1.      why this role is of interest to you

2.      why you are looking for a change from your current job and

3.      how you line up against the key selection criteria in the Candidate Profile.

Note that applications without the cover letter addressing the points above will not be considered.

Job Title: Group Manager Regulatory Pricing and Analysis
Ad Text:
Group Manager Regulatory Pricing and Analysis 

Unique senior leadership opportunity to lead a diverse, high performing team within a complex regulatory environment  
Ongoing, full-time position  
Excellent remuneration and benefits 
Location: Based in Canberra City, with frequent travel to Greenway, Canberra (Onsite parking available at both locations) 

ActewAGL Joint Venture is seeking a highly experienced economic regulatory leader with deep expertise in regulatory pricing, financial modelling, and economic regulation to join the Economic Regulation leadership team as Group Manager Regulatory Pricing and Analysis. 

This is a senior role with direct influence on regulatory strategy, business performance, and long-term value creation across our regulated energy network and retail businesses in the ACT and surrounding NSW. 

This role offers the opportunity to work at the forefront of economic regulation in the energy sector, influencing outcomes that matter to customers, the community and the businesses.  

You’ll be part of a collaborative leadership team, working on complex and meaningful challenges in a dynamic regulatory environment. 

About the role 

Reporting to the General Manager Economic Regulation, you will provide expert regulatory pricing, financial and strategic advice to the Senior Executive Team and the Joint Venture Board. You will lead a team responsible for the development and application of regulatory pricing models and financial analysis to support annual pricing, five yearly regulatory reviews, regulatory policy and broader business improvement initiatives. 

You will play a critical role in shaping the network and retail businesses regulatory strategies, building trusted relationships with regulators and stakeholders, and leading a high performing team.  

A day in the life 

Support the General Manager in the overall leadership and management of the Economic Regulation Division. You will lead, develop, and support the Economic Regulation team, fostering a safe, inclusive, and high performing environment that encourages capability growth and collaboration. 
Lead a high performing team responsible for annual gas and electricity regulatory pricing submissions, regulatory modelling, analysis and strategy development for elements of the five-year regulatory determinations. 
Lead the development of tariff structures and pricing strategies, including for cost recovery of jurisdictional schemes.  
Lead the provision of expert advice on regulatory finance matters, including incentive schemes, economic benchmarking, regulatory depreciation, rates of return, and scenario modelling. 
Lead and oversee the preparation of relevant regulatory policy submissions, advocacy activities, and engagement with regulators and governments. 
Build and maintaining strong, effective relationships with regulators, executives, and the Joint Venture Board. 

Throughout all responsibilities, you will ensure compliance with all legislative, regulatory, and safety obligations, while role modelling the Joint Venture’s commitment to safety, continuous improvement, and future focused leadership. 

About You 

You will bring: 

Tertiary qualifications in Economics, Law, Commerce, Finance or a related discipline  
Strong understanding of energy regulation, microeconomic theory and regulatory frameworks  
Demonstrated experience developing and maintaining sophisticated regulatory pricing models  
Strong experience interpreting regulatory frameworks  
Highly effective communication and leadership skills 
Proven experience presenting regulatory strategy and advice to senior internal and external stakeholders  
Strong stakeholder engagement capability across regulatory, legal, economic and technical environments  
Experience working in complex, multidisciplinary settings.

Ready to make this role your own? 

If this sounds like the kind of opportunity you’ve been looking for, we’d love to hear from you. To apply, log onto our recruitment portal and submit your cover letter and resume outlining the experience, qualifications, and capabilities you can bring to the role.  

For any questions about the role or the application process, please contact Macy Dkhar via email to recruitment@actewagl.com.au

Why join ActewAGL ? 

We’re committed to supporting our people with: 

Ongoing professional development and access to LinkedIn Learning 
Discounted gym memberships and free annual flu vaccinations 
On-site physiotherapy and wellness resources 
Income protection up to 3 years 
Reward and recognition programs 

Considering a move to Canberra? 

Canberra offers a lifestyle that’s hard to beat - easy access to nature, short commute times, vibrant dining and cultural scenes, and proximity to both snowfields and beaches. It’s a city that blends opportunity with quality of life.

The Company
This organisation is a well known organisation operating across community services and education support services, with a large and complex enterprise technology environment. Supporting critical community programs, including education initiatives, they are investing in strengthening their technology capability to better support student services and operational delivery. This includes uplifting IT ownership, governance, and lifecycle management across both infrastructure and business critical applications within their education domain.

This is a six month day rate contract based in Melbourne supporting two key initiatives across IT infrastructure and various technology environments. The Senior Business Analyst will work across infrastructure and asset related uplift, alongside a critical project focused on transitioning ownership of education based applications into the central IT function. A key focus will be assessing the current technology landscape supporting the student lifecycle, defining future state capability, and providing strategic recommendations to improve systems, processes, and overall service delivery within the education environment.

Responsibilities

* Elicit and document current and future state processes, with a strong focus on lifecycle and supporting systems
* Conduct detailed assessment of existing applications and reverse engineer system capabilities across school environments
* Define business, user, and functional requirements aligned to enterprise technology and education domain needs
* Develop business cases, business analysis plans, and governance artefacts to support project delivery
* Provide strategic recommendations to improve technology capability, asset ownership, and service delivery
* Work closely with technology, infrastructure, and on the ground operational stakeholders across multiple domains
* Support project delivery including effort estimation, prioritisation, and execution activities

Candidate Experience
* Experience delivering Business Analysis within enterprise environments across sectors such as not for profit, education, government, or financial services
* Strong exposure to asset management, technology environments, or business critical application uplift within complex organisations
* Proven experience working across IT infrastructure, enterprise applications, and service management environments, with the ability to assess systems and define future state capability

Job Title: Cluster Lead JAPAC, Accounts and Customer Growth for Global Advisory Services
Ad Text:
Cluster Lead – Data, Technology, and Advisory Services (Senior Leadership Role)

Location: Singapore, Tokyo, or Sydney
Function: Advisory Services, Global Data, Technology, and Advisory Services (DTAS)

About the Role

We are seeking inspirational leaders to shape and elevate our Global enterprise Data, Technology, and Advisory Services presence across the United States, Japan, and Asia/Pacific. In an increasingly complex market, our HQ, regional HQ, and enterprise clients face deep challenges. Cluster Leads will serve as trusted partners to these clients, developing deep understanding of their challenges and consistently earning IQVIA the opportunity to support them.

Globally, IQVIA’s clients face daunting challenges: declining R&D productivity, relentless cost pressure, and deteriorating operating economics. Clients urgently need to evolve their models to achieve greater agility, speed, and commercial precision. By combining its unique proprietary data assets, its deep expertise in data architecture and analytics, and its global advisory services, IQVIA has a unique opportunity to be a partner of choice to clients as they navigate this transformation. Earning this opportunity requires strong front-line leaders with a proven ability to build intimacy and trust with senior decision-makers.

As the Cluster Lead for JAPAC, you will serve as the senior client-facing DTAS leader for your region, shaping commercial strategy, driving growth, and ensuring exceptional delivery across a cross-functional team. You will act as a trusted advisor to senior stakeholders, deepen client intimacy, and champion integrated solutions across a range of asset and above-asset priorities. You will bring a partner-level presence, demonstrating the strategic influence, commercial acumen, and people leadership central to the success of professional service firms.

What You’ll Do:

Be the senior face of DTAS for your Cluster, trusted by SVP‑level and other senior stakeholders as a strategic advisor, sounding board, and thought‑partner
Set and own the Technology and Advisory Services growth agenda for your cluster — defining key client priorities, shaping large‑scale opportunities, and driving year‑over‑year expansion and customer satisfaction
Model deep client intimacy and exemplify best‑in‑class client leadership, operating close to your customers, regularly present onsite, and deeply embedded in their strategic priorities
Facilitate cross‑functional collaboration to leverage expertise within Advisory Services (brand strategy, portfolio strategy, value & access, digital health) and the broader DTAS portfolio (data strategy, architecture, outsourcing, technology) — fostering a culture of excellence, collaboration, and innovation.
Ensure high-impact delivery, ensuring work is insight‑rich, strategic, rigorous, and reflective of the high expectations of our customers; further, you will work with your peers across DTAS to ensure a consistent and high standard of deliverable quality and customer experience
Cultivate and nurture top talent, coaching and elevating emerging leaders, providing crisp direction, and creating an environment where teams thrive and grow.
Inform and deepen IQVIA’s Thought Leadership and insight, collaborating with IQVIA’s Thought Leadership organization and front-line practitioners to shape, refine, and communicate strategic insights about critical issues facing our clients
Influence across the enterprise, collaborating closely with other DTAS leaders, GADs, Client Partners, and senior executives to lead and shape client engagement on priority topics and accelerate impact
Own your business, delivering on customer revenue and margin targets

What You Bring

Experience operating at Senior Principal or Partner level in a top-tier consulting environment or comparable internal strategy/advisory leadership.
Broad capabilities and experience across key client priorities, including technology, transformation, commercial strategy, and health care.
A track record of owning and growing major client relationships, with deep comfort engaging senior decision-makers.
Strong commercial acumen: opportunity shaping, complex pursuit leadership, customer value articulation, senior stakeholder orchestration.
A leadership style that inspires confidence, unlocks team performance, and elevates the bar for consulting excellence.
Ability to lead, inspire, and develop multi-disciplinary teams in a dynamic, evolving environment.
Passion for client engagement and a pronounced ability to operate with high presence on client site, building trust through proximity, responsiveness, and insight.
Deep intellectual curiosity and learning agility: a pronounced ability to learn and digest new content and to apply it to customer challenges and priorities
Exceptional communication, storytelling, and influence skills — able to simplify, inspire, and drive alignment across diverse groups.

Position: Business Intelligence Developer (multiple roles)

Employment Type: Permanent Full-Time

Opportunity to work from our modern Chatswood/St Leonards office
Attractive salary from $132,236 up to $150,222 + 12% Super and annual leave loading
Allocated day off per month, salary packaging, discounted fitness passport and more!
Join the team enriching health in millions of ways every day through digital innovation

Applications Close: 11:59pm, 26 March 2026


About Us – Single Digital Patient Record (SDPR), NSW Health
Be part of the team leading the largest digital transformation in Australia’s public health history. NSW Health’s SDPR program is transforming the way patient care is delivered and experienced - creating a single, secure, holistic, and integrated view of patient information across the entire state. 

By connecting every corner of the NSW public health system, this groundbreaking program empowers clinicians and care teams with real-time access to unified patient data - enabling safer, more transparent, and quality care for every person, anywhere. 

Join us now and contribute to the millions of ways we're enriching health every day.
 

About the Opportunity
As a Business Intelligence Developer within our Single Digital Patient Record Implementation Authority (SDPRIA) team, you will help make a real difference in healthcare through the design, development, and support of high-quality analytics tools and reporting solutions used by Local Health Districts and the NSW Ministry of Health. Your work will ensure critical health data is transformed into actionable insights to inform key decisions across the health system. 

This role offers the opportunity to contribute to a range of complex and meaningful analytics projects that support the delivery and improvement of public health services. You’ll work with large-scale health datasets and tools such as SQL, SQL Server Integration Services (SSIS), and business intelligence platforms on Windows. 

In this role, you will:

Develop and maintain responsive, reliable, and maintainable dashboards, reports, extracts, and analytics tools
Analyse user requirements and provide tailored technical solutions to meet business needs
Lead support and incident management activities across SDPR platforms, ensuring high performance and service quality
Implement performance testing and manage change processes for reporting and analytics solutions
Collaborate with stakeholders to deliver easy-to-use, plain language tools that enhance understanding and use of health data 

About You

We are looking for someone who:

Has advanced SQL skills and experience in a large, complex service industry environment
Brings extensive knowledge of complex data models and demonstrated experience working with large and complex databases
Is experienced with analytics tools, SSIS, data warehousing, ETL processes and writing custom SQL queries for reporting and dashboards.
Holds a relevant qualification in Computer Science or a related field
Can work both independently and collaboratively, demonstrating resilience and problem-solving under pressure.
Has experience with version control, change management processes, and requirement gathering for reporting solutions.
Provides technical consultation on upgrades, installations, and modifications related to performance testing and analytics tools.

 

You will succeed in this role if you:

Enjoy working in an environment where no two days are the same.
Are motivated by tight deadlines and problem solving on the go.
Can juggle multiple priorities while staying calm and outcome focused.
Thrive when collaborating with others to deliver results in a fast moving space.

This role may require evening, night, weekend and/or shift work, in accordance with operational needs and Award provisions.
This recruitment may be used to create a Talent Pool for similar future roles (ongoing or temporary) that may arise over the next 18 months.
